Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

fix: correct CoreML ANE narrative — GPU fallback on macOS 26.3

Major paper revision: honestly report that CoreML compute_units=ALL
on macOS 26.3 routes computation to GPU, not ANE (ANE power ≈ 0W).

Key changes:
- Title updated to "CoreML and ANE Private API Prefill"
- Abstract corrected: 5 pipelines, GPU fallback as finding (i)
- Added ANE-LM Batch Dispatch section (268 tok/s, 11.3× speedup)
- Added macOS 26.3 CoreML GPU Fallback section with evidence
- Reframed 2B BF16 "zero overhead" as GPU-to-GPU consistency
- All "ANE batched" labels changed to "CoreML batched"
- Crossover analysis qualified with GPU routing caveat
- Added ANE-LM-Batch-Bench reference
- engine.py: reverted to compute_units=ALL (CPU_AND_NE crashes)
- index.html: updated all claims, stat cards, findings cards
- README.md: updated tables, key findings, conversion notes

commit.message:
feat(analytics): track in-app guide reads as real Ahrefs pageviews

The Ahrefs analytics.js script monkey-patches history.pushState and
fires a pageview for every pushState call. When the GuideReaderPanel
opens, we now push the guide URL into browser history, which Ahrefs
automatically records as a real pageview with the correct URL and title.

This means guide traffic in Ahrefs Web Analytics (Top Pages, visitors,
bounce rate) now includes both direct visits AND in-app panel reads.

- Push guide URL via pushState on panel open (triggers Ahrefs pageview)
- Restore original URL via replaceState on panel close (no extra pageview)
- Browser back button correctly closes the panel
- Update useAppRouting to handle /guide/ paths (prevents 404 on popstate)
- Remove broken window.AhrefsAnalytics.sendEvent call and global declaration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(server): hoist auth barrier and echo tool to module-level statics

Move AUTH_BARRIER OnceLock and auth_barrier_slot() from inside the
http_read_only_snapshot_keeps_request_auth_isolated test function up to
the module-level test statics section, alongside HTTP_OVERLAP_METRICS
and HTTP_OVERLAP_LOCK. Extract http_auth_echo_tool_runtime into a
standalone #[tool]-annotated function so it can be referenced as
HttpAuthEchoToolRuntime (the generated struct name) rather than being
re-declared inside the test body.

This eliminates a nested static + nested function pattern that prevented
the tool from being reused across tests and made the test body harder to
follow. The Barrier::new(3) in the auth_barrier_slot is corrected to
Barrier::new(2), matching the actual number of concurrent HTTP requests
the test dispatches (two overlapping auth-bearing requests).

Also in tasks.rs: capture running_snapshot.is_some() into should_start
before passing the snapshot by value to notify_snapshot(), fixing a
use-after-move where the is_none() check happened after the value had
already been consumed by the notification call.

In tests.rs: update three call sites from ActiveRequestGuard::new() to
ActiveRequestGuard::try_new().expect(), reflecting the API change from
infallible to fallible construction that returns an error when the
request ID is already tracked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Nuevo modulo DriveV2Window con diseno moderno replicado desde Figma Make:
- Sidebar izquierdo (256px): navegacion, filtros por tipo de archivo, indicador de almacenamiento

commit.message:
fix: add direction filtering to prevent message echo

Add `side` parameter to createBridge() so each bridge instance knows
which direction's messages to listen to. Page-side bridges only receive
__to-page__ messages; content-script-side bridges only receive
__to-content__ messages. This prevents a sender from hearing its own
messages (echo bug).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement QR code provisioning flow for camp sync

Backend changes:
- Add CampAuthController with /api/auth/camp/generate-qr and /api/auth/camp/exchange endpoints
- Add CampAuthService for sync code generation and network interface auto-detection
- Fix health endpoint route order in MembersController (health before :id)
- Update startup log to show /api/members/health endpoint

Frontend changes:
- Add QRProvisioningService for orchestrating QR scan → server test → token exchange → sync
- Add "Genereer QR Kode" button to reception dashboard with QR display UI
- Implement ML Kit barcode scanning in home page scanQRToSync()
- Add QRPayload and ProvisioningResult models
- Update QRService with generateCampProvisioningQR() method
- Update ApiService with generateCampQR() endpoint
- Add auto-clear of camp_base_url in reception page to prevent API conflicts
- Use CapacitorHttp instead of fetch() to bypass WebView network restrictions

Android configuration:
- Update network_security_config.xml to allow all cleartext HTTP (offline camp scenario)
- Add extensive debug alerts throughout provisioning flow

Documentation:
- Update Story 1.3 button text: "SKANDEER QR KODE" and "SINKRONISASIE"
- Enhance Test Case 2 with detailed backend setup instructions
- Add Test Cases 11-14 for Raspberry Pi deployment scenarios
- Add comprehensive QR provisioning flow documentation

This implements the full QR-based provisioning workflow where:
1. Staff generates QR from reception dashboard
2. QR contains serverUrls[], syncCode, and campId
3. Devices scan QR and test each URL sequentially
4. First reachable URL is used to exchange code for token
5. Device syncs data over LAN using token
6. Token cleared after successful sync

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Integrate delete/archive with system photo library

Delete now calls MediaLibrary.deleteAssetsAsync() which shows the iOS
system confirmation dialog. Create folder calls createAlbumAsync() to
make a real system album. Archive calls addAssetsToAlbumAsync() to add
photos to the system album. Like remains app-local (no system API).

commit.message:
docs(claude): add Feature Change Protocol

Adds a mandatory 4-step protocol that must run whenever a feature is
added, changed, or removed:
  1. Update affected .proto files and regenerate bindings
  2. Edit all open GitHub issues whose acceptance criteria or technical
     notes are now stale
  3. Create new issues for any module impacted but not yet tracked
  4. Update ARCHITECTURE.md domain model table

Includes a summary table mapping change type → required actions, and
a Quick Reference row for discoverability.

commit.message:
Fix concurrent multi-contact message processing

Previously, all messages were processed sequentially -- if Alice's AI
response took 3s, Bob would wait 3s before his message even started.

Now uses per-contact asyncio locks so:
- Different contacts are processed CONCURRENTLY (3x+ speedup)
- Same contact messages stay SEQUENTIAL (preserves conversation order)
- Zero cross-contamination between contact histories or AI context

Channel dispatches message handling as asyncio tasks instead of
awaiting inline. Orchestrator acquires per-chat_id lock to ensure
same-contact ordering while allowing cross-contact parallelism.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add ManagerAgent V2 with parallel execution and improved routing

## Major Changes

### ManagerAgent V2 (core/agents/manager_v2.py)
- Add true parallel task execution using asyncio.gather()
- Implement DAG-based task graph with dependency support
- Add intelligent intent understanding without hard-coded keywords
- Improve result aggregation with combine_all and last_only strategies
- Fix "repeater" issue by preventing unnecessary clarifications
- Add comprehensive comparison query handling

### Agent Description System (core/agents/descriptions/)
- Add modular agent descriptions in markdown format
- Support YAML frontmatter for routing metadata
- Add descriptions for: chat, crypto, tw_stock, us_stock, forex, commodity, economic agents

### Tools & Routing (core/agents/tools.py, router.py)
- Add forex tools: get_forex_rate, get_all_forex_rates, get_usd_twd_rate
- Add economic tools: market indices, VIX, sector performance
- Add commodity tools: gold/silver ratio, oil analysis
- Improve router to support new agent types

### Prompts (core/agents/prompts/)
- Enhance chat_agent.yaml with result aggregation capabilities
- Update crypto_agent.yaml with comprehensive analysis guidelines

### Tests
- Add interactive user test (test_interactive_user.py)
- Add V2 scenario tests and HITL tests
- Add routing verification tests

### Code Quality
- Fix all ruff lint errors
- Remove unused imports and variables
- Add noqa comments for intentional E402 violations in test files
